title: Expand Trello skill with archive and state-management operations
body:
## Summary
The Trello skill currently supports create/read/list/move/comment/attach/update/archive for a narrow v1 surface, but it is missing state-management operations that users expect for boards, lists, and cards.

## Goal
Add safe, script-first support for archive/close and reopen flows before adding more destructive or admin-heavy features.

## Scope
- board archive/close
- board reopen
- list archive/close if supported cleanly by Trello
- list reopen if supported cleanly by Trello
- card unarchive
- update docs for the new supported operations

## Requirements
- keep the current thin trello_api.py + small script pattern
- preserve explicit failure behavior
- require explicit action flags where an operation is potentially destructive or surprising
- do not add delete behavior in this issue
- do not guess ambiguous board/list/card names

## Acceptance criteria
- new scripts exist for each supported archive/reopen action
- shared client methods are added in skills/trello/scripts/trello_api.py
- name resolution safety remains intact
- tests cover request construction and representative CLI behavior
- skills/trello/SKILL.md documents the new commands

## Notes
This is the best first expansion because it closes real workflow gaps without introducing the risk of permanent deletes or workspace-admin behavior.
